data.title: Guyana accepts production sharing agreements for five offshore oil blocks
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE), QatarEnergy and several other groups have reached agreements with Guyana over terms of production sharing deals to explore five oil and gas offshore blocks, Reuters reported late Wednesday.

A tender launched in 2022 for areas in shallow and deep waters led to offers for eight blocks, part of the government's effort to diversify its energy industry dominated by the Exxon-led (XOM) consortium.

The Exxon (XOM) group, which also is in talks to explore offshore area S8 that is part of the tender, is interested in assessing carbon capture and storage opportunities within the block, the ministry said, according to the report.

A government official said previously that Guyana's cabinet had approved a bid by TotalEnergies (TTE), QatarEnergy and Petronas for exploring shallow water block S4 and negotiations over non-fiscal terms had begun.

data.title: Meta has reportedly fired employees who used meal credits for household shopping
Amylyx Pharmaceuticals reports results from pancreatic disease treatment trial
data.description:
• Amylyx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMLX) announced topline data from the mid-stage trial of AMX0035 in 12 adults living with Wolfram syndrome.
  • The trial showed improvement in pancreatic function, as measured by C-peptide response, following 24 weeks of treatment with AMX0035; worsening is typically expected with disease progression based on natural history studies of Wolfram syndrome.
  • Longer-term data for all participants who have completed Week 36 and Week 48 assessments showed sustained improvement over time.
  • Improvements or stabilization observed across all secondary endpoints, including measures of glycemic control, vision, and patient- and clinician-reported impressions of overall disease burden.
  • AMX0035 was generally well-tolerated in all participants.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow rise after robust results from TSMC and strong retail sales
data.description:
The major averages were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and robust retail sales, helped revive investor sentiments.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.5%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.9%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

U.S. Treasury yields climbed as investors sold off bonds following stronger-than-expected September retail sales data. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.99%.

Initial jobless claims matched and fell to 241K in the past week, while U.S. retail sales climbed more than expected in September. The Philly Fed outlook came in at +10.3 in October.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 9%, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

Also, U.S. industrial production numbers retreat more than expected in September as levels dipped 0.3% M/M. Also on the economic docket, the Fed balance sheet is expected to land later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Dow flirts with record highs and these 5 stocks are sticking out
data.description:
As the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) hovers near record trading highs, see which stocks are leading the benchmark index according to Seeking Alpha’s quant metrics.

As of Thursday, the Dow trades at 43,274.60 points and is higher on the trading year by 14.8%, and is also higher by 4.5% on a much shorter 1-month time frame.

The blue-chip gauge hit an all-time intraday peak two days ago at 43,277.78 points, and closed at a record closing level of 43,077.70 in the previous session.

For reference, Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings system grades stocks based on quantitative measures, such as profitability, momentum and valuation. Quant ratings are given on a scale of 1-5.

Top 5 Dow Components per SA Quant Ratings

No. 5: Apple (AAPL), quant rating of 3.47.

No. 4: Honeywell International (HON), quant rating of 3.69.

No. 3: Travelers Companies (TRV), quant rating of 4.18.

No. 2: Microsoft (MSFT), quant rating of 4.64.

No. 1: American Express Company (AXP), quant rating of 4.94.

Year-to-date performances: AAPL +20.4%, HON +4.4%, TRV +27.5%, MSFT +10.6%, and AXP +50.3%.

As an add-on, the Dow is made up of 30 stocks and the two worst performers of the index from a quant grade vantage point are Intel (INTC) and Boeing (BA) as the two stocks hold scores of 2.59 and 2.72, respectively.

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the Dow (DJI), they may look towards exchange-traded funds that mirror the price movements of the blue-chip index. Some funds worth noting are as below:

Dow ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW).

data.title: XORTX Therapeutics stock falls after pricing $1.5M capital raise
data.description:
XORTX Therapeutics (NASDAQ:XRTX) shares fell around 8% Thursday morning after the pharma firm priced a securities offering to raise $1.5M in gross proceeds.

The company entered into securities purchase agreements with institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 810,810 common shares (or common share equivalents in lieu thereof) in a registered direct offering and common warrants to purchase up to 810,810 common shares in a concurrent private placement at a combined purchase price of $1.85 per common share.

The warrants will have an exercise price of $2.18, will be immediately exercisable and will expire five years from issuance.

The gross proceeds are expected to be approximately $1.5M; net proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: NUZEE announces departure of former co-CEO Randy Weaver
data.description:
• NUZEE (NASDAQ:NUZE) on Thursday said Randy Weaver, former co-chief executive officer, has departed from the company effective August 30, 2024.
  • Jianshuang Wang, the chairwoman of the Board of Directors and CEO, will continue to serve as the CEO.
  • The company said it has established offices in Singapore, Hong Kong, Mainland China and other regions to develop its global business.
  • The company has moved its U.S. operations from California to Florida.
  • NUZE +52.10% premarket to $0.5415.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ASP Isotopes enriches Ytterbium-176 in First Quantum commissioning phase
data.description:
ASP Isotopes (NASDAQ:ASPI) +5.4% pre-market Thursday after saying it accelerated the commissioning phase of its first Quantum Enrichment plant and produced the first semi-finished material of enriched Ytterbium-176.

The company initially expected the commissioning phase would take 3-6 months, but enriched Ytterbium-176 was produced during the first six weeks.

ASP Isotopes (ASPI) said it expects to be able to achieve a 99.75% enrichment for Ytterbium-176 and offer highly enriched Ytterbium-176 for commercial sale in 2025.

The company said it expects to proceed with plans to construct Nickel-64 and Lithium-6/7 Quantum Enrichment plants with targeted production during 2025, while continuing discussions with government authorities for authorization to construct HALEU uranium enrichment facilities using Quantum Enrichment.

data.title: PAI Partners raises offer for Sanofi's consumer unit - reports
data.description:
PAI Partners increased its offer for Sanofi's (NASDAQ:SNY) consumer health unit.

The private equity shop raised its offer by about €200 million ($217 million) for the Opella business and pledged to keep the unit's headquarters and other key operations in France, according to media accounts, including a Bloomberg report on Thursday. PAI Partners is working with sovereign wealth funds Abu Dhabi Investment Authority and Singapore’s GIC Pte on the bid for the Sanofi unit.

The news comes after Sanofi on Friday confirmed it entered into talks with U.S. private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice (CD&R) to sell a controlling 50% stake in its consumer health business, Opella. Bloomberg reported last week that Sanofi was close to selling a majority stake in Opella for around EUR 15B, or $16.4B.

On Wednesday, Reuters reported that Sanofi’s (SNY) two largest unions in France reportedly plan to go on strike over the proposed sale of the Opella unit. At issue are concerns that the deal could result in the loss of jobs and domestic production of certain medications like the pain reliever Doliprane.
